chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: Welcome back to 100 days of code.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: In today's video, we'll talk about modules.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: And what is PIP?

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: Modules are basically used to borrow other people's code.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: That means you can use someone else's code in your Python program

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: with the help of modules.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: There are two types of modules.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: We have built-in modules.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: And we have external modules

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: which are installed with the help of PIP.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: External modules are those codes that people have written

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: and they want you to use their code in your Python program.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: Let's say I'm using this phone.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: I don't know what's going on inside this phone

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: and how it's working.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: But I'm still able to use this phone.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: This is an external module for me.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: I don't know what its implementation is.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: But I'm able to use it.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: Built-in modules, on the other hand,

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: are those modules that are shipped with the Python programming language.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: That means you don't need to install them externally.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: We use modules because we don't want to code every basic thing from scratch.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: If you're working in a big machine learning program,

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: if you're making a self-driving car with artificial intelligence,

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: you won't want to write a program to get an average of 10 numbers.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: Or you can write a program to get a geometric mean of 10 numbers.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: It can have a human error.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: This code is written inside the modules.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: It's well-tested.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: It's written by experts.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: And because a lot of people have used it,

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: there are very few chances of errors.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: Now, if you want, you can write a program from scratch

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: for your understanding.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: But if you use modules,

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: you'll save time and effort.

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: And you'll be able to work on a big thing.

text: Welcome back to 100 days of code. In today's video, we'll talk about modules. And what is PIP? Modules are basically used to borrow other people's code. That means you can use someone else's code in your Python program with the help of modules. There are two types of modules. We have built-in modules. And we have external modules which are installed with the help of PIP. External modules are those codes that people have written and they want you to use their code in your Python program. Let's say I'm using this phone. I don't know what's going on inside this phone and how it's working. But I'm still able to use this phone. This is an external module for me. I don't know what its implementation is. But I'm able to use it. Built-in modules, on the other hand, are those modules that are shipped with the Python programming language. That means you don't need to install them externally. We use modules because we don't want to code every basic thing from scratch. If you're working in a big machine learning program, if you're making a self-driving car with artificial intelligence, you won't want to write a program to get an average of 10 numbers. Or you can write a program to get a geometric mean of 10 numbers. It can have a human error. This code is written inside the modules. It's well-tested. It's written by experts. And because a lot of people have used it, there are very few chances of errors. Now, if you want, you can write a program from scratch for your understanding.
